rationale: Operator-usability walkthrough of the thinned skills largely holds together: run -> tally flow composes cleanly, the panel-create/panel-tally invocations match the real CLI grammar exactly (built cmd/mindspec, ran `panel create --help` — flags identical to the skill's documented invocation), the halt-recovery re-point to `mindspec panel create <slug> --spec <id> --target <ref> --round <N+1>` is coherent and even echoes the binary's own recovery-hint wording verbatim (cmd/mindspec/panel.go:533), and ms-bead-cycle's references to '/ms-panel-run step 0' / '/ms-panel-tally' still line up with what the thinned skills actually do. One real gap: the skill never tells the operator to capture/use `panel create`'s own printed `panel directory: <dir>` line (cmd/mindspec/panel.go:140, added in Bead 4) as the authoritative location for the BRIEF/verdict files. Instead every downstream step (filling the BRIEF stub, writing codex prompts, verifying the six JSON files landed) re-derives the path from the skill's own prose convention note ('<spec-dir>/reviews/<panel-slug>/'). That prose is pre-existing (unchanged since before Bead 4/5) and only covers the flat-layout branch of `panelDirFor` (cmd/mindspec/panel.go:271-280) — it never mentions the legacy non-flat fallback (`<root>/review/<slug>`) that `panelDirFor` still resolves to on an unflattened repo. Duplicating path logic the CLI already resolves and prints is exactly the kind of drift risk this bead's mechanization is otherwise closing elsewhere (round/SHA co-bump, decision matrix). Not load-bearing for the common case (the prose and panelDirFor agree on flat layout), so not a hard block, but a concrete, cheap fix.
concrete_changes_required: In ms-panel-run Step 0 (both plugins/ and .claude/ copies), after the `mindspec panel create ...` invocation, add a line instructing the operator to capture the command's reported `panel directory: <dir>` line and use that path directly for the BRIEF stub, codex prompt files, and verdict-file existence checks — rather than re-deriving the path solely from the `<spec-dir>` convention note above it.
Optionally note in the same convention block that `panelDirFor` also has a non-flat legacy fallback (`<root>/review/<slug>`) so the prose doesn't silently assume every repo is on the flat layout.
findings: cmd/mindspec/panel.go:140 prints `panel directory: %s` on every `panel create` call — this output exists specifically so callers don't have to re-implement panelDirFor's layout-aware resolution, but ms-panel-run/SKILL.md never references it; the skill still has the operator compute the path from the (pre-existing, Bead-5-unchanged) prose convention note at lines 19-26.

panelDirFor (cmd/mindspec/panel.go:271-280) has two branches: flat -> `<spec-dir>/reviews/<slug>` (which the skill's convention note documents), and non-flat -> `<root>/review/<slug>` (which the skill's note explicitly claims 'no longer drives the gate' — true for the flat case, but the CLI code path still exists and the skill gives the operator no way to know which one applies without inspecting `workspace.DetectLayout` themselves).

Everything else checked clean: `diff -q` confirms both skill mirrors are byte-identical to their plugins/ originals; grep gates (no `| Condition | Action |` table, no quoted `"reviewed_head_sha"`, no 'artifact-gate finding may set', `## Artifact gates` present, `Slot lens defaults` present) pass on both copies; all judgment sections named in the BRIEF (Launch the panel, Codex failure detection, Working directory matters, Slot lens defaults, Anti-patterns / Consolidate, Artifact gates, After a halt, Escape hatch) are present verbatim; the intro-paragraph edit to ms-panel-tally is a necessary accuracy fix given the deleted matrix steps, and leaving the YAML `description:` untouched is fine — it still describes the skill at the catalog-summary altitude ('single decision authority... apply the decision matrix') and no grep gate touches it.

runbook.md's new Maintenance Notes entry is accurate but incomplete: it explicitly names only 'Slot lens defaults' and 'Anti-patterns' as ms-panel-run's surviving judgment sections, folding 'Launch the panel', 'Codex failure detection', and 'Working directory matters' into an unenumerated 'unchanged' — a minor completeness gap in the doc-sync prose, not a defect.
